stations.name: Cannon Hill railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Within the suburb. Most Cannon Hill streets are within a 5 to 15 minute walk of the station.

trains.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 14 to 18 minutes to Central station off-peak.

buses.note: Bus routes serve Cannon Hill via Wynnum Road, Creek Road, and Stanley Street East.

driving.motorway_access: Direct Gateway Motorway access via the Cannon Hill interchange. Story Bridge for inner-CBD travel.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 10 to 14 minutes to Brisbane CBD off-peak via Wynnum Road or Story Bridge.
driving.airport_access_note: Brisbane Airport approximately 10 to 12 minutes off-peak via Gateway.

connections: Norman Creek bikeway connecting toward the river system
Walking access to the K-Mart Plaza precinct from most streets

secondary_state.name: Balmoral State High School
secondary_state.note: Common catchment for many Cannon Hill streets.

lifestyle.shops_dining_note: Cannon Hill K-Mart Plaza is the major retail centre with supermarket, big-format retail, cinema, and casual dining. Daily groceries and local cafes through the precinct. Bulimba and Hawthorne village strips are within seven to ten minutes by car for the broader inner-east dining range.
lifestyle.lifestyle_note: Cannon Hill combines train station convenience, big-format retail on the doorstep, and established residential character. The combination is rare in inner east Brisbane.

pockets.name: Station-adjacent
pockets.character: Streets within walking distance of Cannon Hill station. Mix of character homes and post-war stock. The most contested pocket for transport-focused buyers.

pockets.name: Family streets
pockets.character: Quieter residential streets away from the arterials. Established family-home stock, larger blocks, and the suburb's traditional character home market.

pockets.name: Wynnum Road corridor
pockets.character: Closer to the arterial. Convenient for transport but trades on a different value proposition than the quieter family streets.

buyer_profile.note: Buyers moving up from Tingalpa, Murarrie, or further east. The train station and the K-Mart Plaza precinct are the practical drivers for this segment.
Parents targeting the Cannon Hill catchment corridor and the family-suburb feel of the quieter residential streets.
Sydney and Melbourne buyers drawn by the airport proximity and the inner-east transport convenience.
Train station and arterial proximity supports rental demand. Cannon Hill consistently produces yield numbers that work for investor buyers.

verify_flags: Cannon Hill State School catchment boundary
